name: Around Alora with a visit to the Arab castle - circular
name_original: Alrededor de Álora con visita en el castillo árabe – circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Álora offers diverse terrain within the Guadalhorce Valley, characterized by fertile agricultural fields, citrus groves, and olive trees. The region features a mix of flat valley routes and challenging ascents into surrounding mountain ranges like the Sierra del Valle del Abdalajis and Sierra de Aguas. Cyclists can experience routes that connect charming white villages and provide panoramic views of the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes gorge. This landscape provides varied cycling terrain suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes in Álora The most popular road cycling route is Around Alora with a visit to the Arab castle - circular, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the historic Álora Castle and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Álora Castle – Raja Ancha Recreation Area loop from Álora, a moderate 46.0 miles (74.0 km) path. This route traverses the valley, offering scenic views of the castle and the natural landscape. Local road cyclists also love the View of Álora Castle – Coín loop from Las Mellizas, a 62.6 miles (100.7 km) trail leading through the Guadalhorce Valley and towards Coín, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around Álora is defined by wide valleys, mountain ascents, and traditional white villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides through agricultural areas to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. The routes in Álora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Álora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Álora?
A: Road cycling around Álora offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find flat routes winding through the fertile Guadalhorce Valley, characterized by citrus groves and olive trees. For a challenge, there are significant ascents into mountain ranges like the Sierra del Valle del Abdalajis and Sierra de Aguas, providing varied cycling experiences for all fitness levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Álora region?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around Álora, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, there are easy routes perfect for a relaxed ride. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939299308/around-alora-with-a-visit-to-the-arab-castle-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Alora with a visit to the Arab castle - circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22 km long and offers pleasant views of the historic Álora Castle and the surrounding countryside.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I might see on a road cycling route near Álora?
A: Road cycling routes in Álora often feature stunning natural and historical landmarks. You can expect panoramic views of the Guadalhorce Valley, the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes gorge, and the ancient Álora Castle. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaitanejo Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt; and offer glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalhorce River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32045051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Álora Castle – Coín loop from Las Mellizas&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains over 100 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10300316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Caminito del Rey – View of El Burgo loop from Las Mellizas&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 2,300 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Álora?
A: The road cycling routes in Álora are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 out of 5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from fertile valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the charm of passing through traditional white villages.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Álora?
A: Yes, many routes around Álora are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939299308/around-alora-with-a-visit-to-the-arab-castle-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Alora with a visit to the Arab castle - circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is popular for its scenic views and historical interest.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Caminito del Rey or offer views of it?
A: Yes, some challenging road cycling routes offer views of the iconic Caminito del Rey and the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes gorge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey – View of Caminito del Rey loop from Las Mellizas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that brings you close to this impressive natural feature.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Álora?
A: Álora's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months. These seasons provide ideal conditions for enjoying both valley rides and mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%81lora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cervantes&lt;/a&gt; is a key viewpoint in Álora, providing extensive vistas of the town, the surrounding countryside, and the Guadalhorce Valley. Routes often incorporate such scenic spots, allowing cyclists to take in the breathtaking landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural parks or protected areas are near Álora for cycling?
A: Álora is surrounded by significant natural areas. The El Chorro Nature Park, home to the Desfiladero de los Gaitanes, is nearby, offering rugged landscapes. Ardales National Park, a short drive away, features towering cliffs and clear lakes. These parks contribute to the region's diverse and scenic cycling environment.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring traditional 'pueblos blancos'?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling routes in Álora are designed to connect and pass through charming 'pueblos blancos' (white villages). This allows riders to experience the authentic Andalusian culture, traditional architecture, and local life, adding a cultural dimension to their cycling adventure.
tips: This viewpoint located at the foot of the Arab castle of Alora offers us a magnificent view of the town, the Guadalhorce river valley and the mountains.

Álora Castle, located on the Cerro de las Torres, has a rich history that spans from its Phoenician and Roman origins to its reconstruction by the Arabs, who turned it into a residence and citadel. The structure has undergone various stages of construction, from the Emirate to the Kingdoms of Taifas.

The parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación stands on the old mosque, built between 1600 and 1699. The castle was damaged in the earthquake of 1680 and, since then, served as a cemetery. Although only two partially reconstructed towers and a horseshoe arch in the wall remain standing today, the site was declared a National Historic Monument in 1931.
